_meta.schema: appearances / titles / titleYears / finals / semifinals / bestFinish / firstAppearance / blurb
_meta.source: FIFA World Cup historical records through 2022

argentina.blurb: Defending champions. Won Qatar 2022 led by Messi. Three-time winners — joint third in all-time titles.

brazil.blurb: The only nation to play every World Cup. Five-time champions — most successful country in the tournament's history.

france.blurb: Won at home in 1998 and again in Russia 2018. Runners-up in 2022 — back-to-back finals.

spain.blurb: Tiki-taka era peaked in South Africa 2010 — won every knockout 1–0. New golden generation rising again.

germany.blurb: Four-time champions, joint second with Italy. Most semifinals of any nation — perennial contender.

england.blurb: Hosted and won in 1966. Semifinalists in 2018, quarter-final exits in 2022 — golden generation chasing first title since.

portugal.blurb: Eusébio's third-place run in 1966 remains the high point. Ronaldo's swan song in 2026.

netherlands.blurb: Total Football pioneers — three final defeats. Quarter-final exits in 2014 and 2022.

belgium.blurb: Golden generation's best finish was 3rd in 2018. New cycle under García.

japan.blurb: Four R16 exits in five attempts since 1998. Beat Germany and Spain in 2022 group stage — Asia's bellwether.
